possible_discriminator: Vary system expressive power: M01 predicts circularity depth correlates with expressiveness; M02 predicts same convergence regardless.
notes: Both predict circularity, but M01 attributes it to self-reference limits and M02 to mathematical convergence. M01 is domain-independent; M02 is procedure-dependent.

possible_discriminator: Change method: M01 predicts any method within the system hits the same wall; M03 predicts different methods give different results.
notes: Fundamentally different: M01 says the system cannot avoid recursion; M03 says the method imposes it. Clear experimental separation possible.

possible_discriminator: Vary observer stance: M01 predicts all stances within the system hit circularity; M04 predicts different stances produce different recursion.
notes: Related: both involve the relationship between investigator and investigated. M01 emphasizes system limits; M04 emphasizes inseparability.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information source: M05 predicts this breaks the recursion; M01 predicts the limitation is inherent regardless.
notes: Different causal mechanisms: M01 = self-reference bound; M05 = information bound. May produce similar surface behavior.

possible_discriminator: Apply large perturbation: M06 predicts possible escape; M01 predicts escape requires external reference (not same as perturbation).
notes: Different emphasis: M01 = structural limit; M06 = dynamical trap. Potentially compatible descriptions of same situation at different levels.

possible_discriminator: Reformulate question at different level: M07 predicts recursion disappears; M01 predicts recursion reappears at new level if system remains self-referential.
notes: M01 and M07 share the idea that recursion is a symptom, not the phenomenon. M01 says the system is limited; M07 says the question is wrong.

possible_discriminator: Evaluate whether the recursion produces new information (M01: yes, generative frustration) vs is stable and complete (M08). Scale-invariance test.
notes: Directly opposed: M01 says recursion is failure; M08 says it is success. Cannot both be correct in same sense.

possible_discriminator: Change method while holding operation properties fixed: M03 predicts different result; M02 predicts new fixed point for new operation.
notes: Close cousins: M02 emphasizes mathematical necessity of convergence; M03 emphasizes contingency of method choice. Could collapse if method choice is shown to be forced.

possible_discriminator: Vary observer relationship: M02 predicts convergence independent of observer; M04 predicts result changes with observer.
notes: Potentially compatible: M02 describes procedure behavior; M04 describes observer relation. M04 predicts M02's operation is observer-dependent.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information sources: M05 predicts new structure; M02 predicts same fixed point regardless.
notes: Different mechanisms: M02 = guaranteed convergence; M05 = information depletion. Same surface (all candidates collapse) but different cause.

possible_discriminator: Check trajectory behavior after convergence: M02 predicts stable rest; M06 predicts ongoing cycling around attractor.
notes: Different dynamic predictions: M02 converges to rest; M06 cycles. Distinguishable by trajectory shape over extended phases.

possible_discriminator: Reformulate question: M07 predicts recursion disappears; M02 predicts procedure finds a fixed point of the new operation.
notes: M02 says the procedure does what procedures do (find fixed points); M07 says the procedure shouldn't be doing this at all.

possible_discriminator: Check informativeness of result: M02 sees recursion as convergent limit (procedure exhausted its utility); M08 sees it as complete discovery.
notes: Different evaluation of same formal fact: M02 says procedure exhausted; M08 says procedure succeeded.

possible_discriminator: Hold method fixed, vary observer stance: M03 predicts same result (method determines); M04 predicts different result (observer matters).
notes: Strong overlap: both say the result depends on how you approach the target. M03 emphasizes method design; M04 emphasizes observer position.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information without changing method: M05 predicts new structure; M03 predicts same method-dependent result.
notes: Different intervention predictions: M03 says change method; M05 says find new data. Could be distinguished by which intervention changes the result.

possible_discriminator: Apply small perturbations: M03 predicts method still determines result; M06 predicts return to same attractor. Vary method: M03 predicts different results; M06 predicts same attractor.
notes: M03 predicts method change changes everything; M06 predicts small method changes are absorbed. Distinguishable by perturbation size effect.

possible_discriminator: Change method within same framework vs change framework: M07 requires framework change; M03 predicts method change within framework suffices.
notes: M03 allows escape by method refinement within framework; M07 requires framework change. Distinguishable by depth of change needed.

possible_discriminator: Method-independence test: M03 predicts result depends on method choice; M08 predicts recursion is method-independent (it IS the phenomenon).
notes: M03 sees recursion as contingent on method; M08 sees it as necessary. Directly opposed.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information sources: M05 predicts new structure; M04 predicts observer relation still colors any new information.
notes: Compatible in principle: observer relation determines what counts as information. Distinguishable by what intervention resolves recursion.

possible_discriminator: Change observer stance vs perturb dynamics: M04 predicts stance change (even small) changes result; M06 predicts small perturbations are absorbed by attractor.
notes: M04 predicts observer stance change (potentially small) changes result; M06 predicts small changes are absorbed. Directly testable.

possible_discriminator: Change observer stance vs reformulate question: M04 predicts stance change suffices; M07 predicts deeper category-level change needed.
notes: Both involve 'changing perspective' but at different levels: observer stance vs conceptual framework. Potentially nested.

possible_discriminator: Vary observer: M04 predicts recursion changes with observer; M08 predicts recursion is invariant (it IS the phenomenon).
notes: Opposed: M04 says recursion is observer-relative; M08 says it is invariant. Directly testable by varying observer.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information: M05 predicts this changes result; M06 predicts new info within basin is absorbed by attractor.
notes: Different mechanisms: information depletion vs dynamical entrapment. Same surface (all look same). Distinguishable by whether new info changes result.

possible_discriminator: Introduce new information vs new framework: M05 predicts new info from any source suffices; M07 predicts framework change needed (new info within same framework insufficient).
notes: Different: M05 says new info (any source) suffices; M07 says framework change needed. New info within same framework distinguishes them.

possible_discriminator: New information test: M05 predicts recursion is artifact of depletion; M08 predicts recursion persists even with new information.
notes: Opposed on whether recursion is artifact or essence. Test: does new information produce non-recursive structure?

possible_discriminator: Perturb dynamics vs reformulate question: M06 predicts large dynamic perturbation escapes; M07 predicts framework change escapes (dynamic perturbation may not suffice).
notes: Both predict escape is possible but via different routes (dynamic perturbation vs framework change). Distinguishable by which works.

possible_discriminator: Check whether recursion is stable and informative (M08) vs a trap preventing progress (M06). Scale-invariance test.
notes: Opposed: M06 says recursion is a trap to escape; M08 says it is the found phenomenon. Normative tension.

possible_discriminator: Reformulate question: M07 predicts recursion disappears with correct framing; M08 predicts recursion persists as it IS the phenomenon.
notes: Directly opposed on whether recursion is a symptom of error (M07) or the correct answer (M08). Most fundamental split in the landscape.
